# SWO DNS Query Receiver

## Overview

The DNS Query receiver performs DNS lookups against configured servers and domains, emitting metrics about query response time and result codes. It is the OpenTelemetry replacement for the Telegraf `dns_query` input plugin, part of the Telegraf Conversion initiative ([NH-133332](https://swicloud.atlassian.net/browse/NH-133332)).

For each `(server, domain)` pair, the receiver emits one `ResourceMetrics` containing three gauge metrics. Queries are performed concurrently — one goroutine per pair — and all results are collected before a scrape cycle completes.

For full metric definitions see [documentation.md](./documentation.md).

## Configuration

```yaml
receivers:
  dnsquery:
    # Required: list of DNS servers to query (IP or hostname)
    servers:
      - "8.8.8.8"
      - "1.1.1.1"

    # Domains to resolve. Default: ["."] (root)
    domains:
      - "."
      - "example.com"

    # DNS record type. Default: NS
    # Allowed: A, AAAA, ANY, CNAME, MX, NS, PTR, SOA, SPF, SRV, TXT
    record_type: NS

    # Network protocol. Default: udp
    # Allowed: udp, tcp
    network: udp

    # DNS server port. Default: 53
    port: 53

    # Query timeout. Default: 2s
    timeout: 2s

    # How often to collect metrics. Default: 60s
    collection_interval: 60s
```

### Configuration fields

| Field | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `servers` | `[]string` | — | Yes | DNS servers to query. Each entry is an IP address or hostname. |
| `domains` | `[]string` | `["."]` | No | Domains to resolve. The root domain `"."` queries for root NS records. |
| `record_type` | `string` | `NS` | No | DNS record type to query. Allowed: `A`, `AAAA`, `ANY`, `CNAME`, `MX`, `NS`, `PTR`, `SOA`, `SPF`, `SRV`, `TXT`. |
| `network` | `string` | `udp` | No | Transport protocol. Allowed: `udp`, `tcp`. |
| `port` | `int` | `53` | No | DNS server port. Must be between 1 and 65535. |
| `timeout` | `duration` | `2s` | No | Per-query timeout. Must be a positive duration. |
| `collection_interval` | `duration` | `60s` | No | How frequently to run the scrape cycle. |

## Metrics

| Metric | Type | Unit | Value type | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `dns_query.query_time_ms` | Gauge | ms | Double | DNS query response time in milliseconds. Zero on timeout or error. |
| `dns_query.result_code` | Gauge | 1 | Int | Outcome of the DNS query: `0`=success, `1`=timeout, `2`=error. |
| `dns_query.rcode_value` | Gauge | 1 | Int | Numeric DNS response code (rcode). `0`=NOERROR, `3`=NXDOMAIN, etc. Zero on timeout or error. |

## Metric Attributes

All three metrics carry the same five data-point attributes.

| Attribute | Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dns_query.server` | string | The DNS server that was queried. | `8.8.8.8` |
| `dns_query.domain` | string | The domain that was queried. | `example.com` |
| `dns_query.record_type` | string | The DNS record type that was queried. | `NS` |
| `dns_query.result` | string | The query outcome. | `success`, `timeout`, `error` |
| `dns_query.rcode` | string | The DNS response code name. Empty on timeout or error. | `NOERROR`, `NXDOMAIN`, `SERVFAIL` |

## Resource Attributes

Each `ResourceMetrics` is emitted per `(server, domain)` pair and carries three resource attributes.

| Attribute | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sw.otelcol.dnsquery.server` | string | The DNS server that was queried. |
| `sw.otelcol.dnsquery.domain` | string | The domain that was queried. |
| `sw.otelcol.dnsquery.record_type` | string | The DNS record type that was queried. |

## Result codes

The `dns_query.result_code` metric and the `dns_query.result` attribute encode the high-level outcome of each query.

| Value | Name | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `0` | success | The DNS server responded. The `dns_query.rcode_value` carries the DNS rcode (which may itself indicate an error like NXDOMAIN). |
| `1` | timeout | The query timed out before a response was received. `query_time_ms` and `rcode_value` are both `0`. |
| `2` | error | A non-timeout error occurred (e.g., connection refused, resolution failure). `query_time_ms` and `rcode_value` are both `0`. |

## DNS Response Codes (rcode_value)

When `dns_query.result_code` is `0` (success), `dns_query.rcode_value` holds the numeric DNS rcode from the server response. Common values:

| Value | Name | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `0` | NOERROR | Query completed successfully. |
| `1` | FORMERR | DNS server could not interpret the query. |
| `2` | SERVFAIL | DNS server failed to complete the request. |
| `3` | NXDOMAIN | The queried domain does not exist. |
| `5` | REFUSED | DNS server refused to respond to the query. |

## Example configuration

```yaml
receivers:
  dnsquery:
    servers:
      - "8.8.8.8"
      - "1.1.1.1"
    domains:
      - "."
      - "google.com"
    record_type: NS
    network: udp
    port: 53
    timeout: 2s
    collection_interval: 60s

service:
  pipelines:
    metrics:
      receivers: [dnsquery]
      processors: []
      exporters: []
```
